description_long: This is a rustic, cylindrical wooden bucket with a dark, weathered finish, reinforced by two wide, metal bands—one near the top rim and one at the base. The wood staves are vertically aligned and show natural grain and texture, giving the bucket an aged, utilitarian look. The top rim is uneven and slightly jagged, suggesting hand-craftsmanship or wear from use. A metal bail handle, attached with metal brackets, arches over the top and is secured with visible rivets, while a short length of rope or leather is tied to one side, possibly for hanging or carrying. The metal bands and handle have a tarnished, slightly corroded appearance, indicating exposure to the elements. The interior appears hollow and open, suitable for carrying water, tools, or other materials in an urban or outdoor setting.
description: A weathered wooden bucket with metal bands and a metal bail handle, featuring a rustic, aged appearance and visible wood grain. The bucket is open-topped and reinforced for durability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden staves, metal bands, and a clear view of the open top with no handle visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the metal bail handle and its attachment points, as well as a rope or leather tie on the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the continuous metal banding and the open top, with the handle less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the handle and its attachment more visible, along with the uneven rim and reinforced metal bands.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
